2606.22085.title: Can Reasoning Models Detect Changes to their Chains of Thought?
2606.22085.paper_summary: There are many reasons one may want to edit a model's chain of thought (CoT) -- e.g., to prefill it with reasoning from a stronger model or to remove steps that may yield unsafe outputs. The success of these interventions plausibly depends on a model's inability to notice them, as the model may alter its behavior if it suspects tampering. In this work, we study whether recent reasoning models are able to detect such interventions on their CoTs under a variety of conditions: both during reasoning and after it, and when prefilled both with their own CoTs and with those of other models. Broadly, we find that (i) models exhibit only very modest detection accuracy; (ii) models struggle to identify *how* their CoT was modified; and (iii) models are about as good at detecting changes to their own CoTs as to those of other models.
2606.22085.paper_summary_zh: 有許多原因可能使人們想要編輯模型的思考鏈（CoT）——例如，將其預填充為來自更強模型的推理，或移除可能產生不安全輸出的步驟。這些干預的成功可能取決於模型是否無法察覺到它們，因為如果模型懷疑被篡改，它可能會改變其行為。在這項工作中，我們研究最近的推理模型是否能在各種條件下檢測到其CoT上的這些干預：無論是在推理過程中還是在推理之後，以及在預填充自己CoT和其他模型的CoT時。總體而言，我們發現（i）模型的檢測準確性僅非常有限；（ii）模型難以識別*如何*修改其CoT；以及（iii）模型檢測自己CoT的變更和其他模型CoT的變更的能力大致相當。
2606.22085.author: Sathvik Napa et.al.
2606.22085.authors: Sathvik Napa, Utkarsh Singh, Chengyuan Xue, Miriam Wanner, William Walden
